(fnName) => `CallExpression[callee.name=${fnName}]`;\\n4: const AnyFunction = 'FunctionDeclaration, FunctionExpression, ArrowFunctionExpression';\\n5: \\n6: function checkProperties(context, node) {\\n7:   if (\\n8:     node.type === 'CallExpression' &&\\n9:     node.callee.name === 'ObjectGetOwnPropertyDescriptors'\\n10:   ) {\\n11:     context.report({\\n12:       node,\\n13:       message:\\n14:         'Property descriptors inherits from the Object prototype, therefore are subject to prototype pollution',\\n15:     });\\n16:   }\\n17:   if (node.type !== 'ObjectExpression') return;\\n18:   for (const { key, value } of node.properties) {\\n19:     if (\\n20:       key != null && value != null &&\\n21:       !(key.type === 'Identifier' && key.name === '__proto__') &&\\n22:       !(key.type === 'Literal' && key.value === '__proto__')\\n23:     ) {\\n24:       checkPropertyDescriptor(context, value);\\n25:     }\\n26:   }\\n27: }\\n28: \\n29: function isNullPrototypeObjectExpression(node) {\\n30:   if (node.type !== 'ObjectExpression') return;\\n31: \\n32:   for (const { key, value } of node.properties) {\\n33:     if (\\n34:       key != null && value != null &&\\n35:       ((key.type === 'Identifier' && key.name === '__proto__') ||\\n36:         (key.type === 'Literal' && key.value === '__proto__')) &&\\n37:       value.type === 'Literal' && value.value === null\\n38:     ) {\\n39:       return true;\\n40:     }\\n41:   }\\n42:   return false;\\n43: }\\n44: \\n45: function checkPropertyDescriptor(context, node) {\\n46:   if (\\n47:     node.type === 'CallExpression' &&\\n48:     (node.callee.name === 'ObjectGetOwnPropertyDescriptor' ||\\n49:      node.callee.name === 'ReflectGetOwnPropertyDescriptor')\\n50:   ) {\\n51:     context.report({\\n52:       node,\\n53:       message:\\n54:         'Property descriptors inherits from the Object prototype, therefore are subject to prototype pollution',\\n55:       suggest: [{\\n56:         desc: 'Wrap the property descriptor in a null-prototype object',\\n57:         fix(fixer) {\\n58:           return [\\n59:             fixer.insertTextBefore(node, '{ __proto__: null,...'),\\n60:             fixer.insertTextAfter(node, ' }'),\\n61:           ];\\n62:         },\\n63:       }],\\n64:     });\\n65:   }\\n66:   if (isNullPrototypeObjectExpression(node) === false) {\\n67:     context.report({\\n68:       node,\\n69:       message: 'Must use null-prototype object for property descriptors',\\n70:     });\\n71:   }\\n72: }\\n73: \\n74: function createUnsafeStringMethodReport(context, name, lookedUpProperty) {\\n75:   const lastDotPosition = '$String.prototype.'.length;\\n76:   const unsafePrimordialName = `StringPrototype${name.charAt(lastDotPosition).toUpperCase()}${name.slice(lastDotPosition + 1, -1)}`;\\n77:   return {\\n78:     [CallExpression(unsafePrimordialName)](node) {\\n79:       context.report({\\n80:         node,\\n81:         message: `${name} looks up the ${lookedUpProperty} property on the first argument`,\\n82:       });\\n83:     },\\n84:   };\\n85: }\\n86: \\n87: function createUnsafeStringMethodOnRegexReport(context, name, lookedUpProperty) {\\n88:   const dotPosition = 'Symbol.'.length;\\n89:   const safePrimordialName = `RegExpPrototypeSymbol${lookedUpProperty.charAt(dotPosition).toUpperCase()}${lookedUpProperty.slice(dotPosition + 1)}`;\\n90:   const lastDotPosition = '$String.prototype.'.length;\\n91:   const unsafePrimordialName = `StringPrototype${name.charAt(lastDotPosition).toUpperCase()}${name.slice(lastDotPosition + 1, -1)}`;\\n92:   return {\\n93:     [[\\n94:       `${CallExpression(unsafePrimordialName)}[arguments.1.type=Literal][arguments.1.regex]`,\\n95:       `${CallExpression(unsafePrimordialName)}[arguments.1.type=NewExpression][arguments.1.callee.name=RegExp]`,\\n96:     ].join(',')](node) {\\n97:       context.report({\\n98:         node,\\n99:         message: `${name} looks up the ${lookedUpProperty} property of the passed regex, use ${safePrimordialName} directly`,\\n100:       });\\n101:     },\\n102:   };\\n103: }\\n104: \\n105: module.exports = {\\n106:   meta: { hasSuggestions: true },\\n107:   create(context) {\\n108:     return {\\n109:       [CallExpression(/^(Object|Reflect)DefinePropert(ies|y)$/)](node) {\\n110:         switch (node.callee.name) {\\n111:           case 'ObjectDefineProperties':\\n112:             checkProperties(context, node.arguments[1]);\\n113:             break;\\n114:           case 'ReflectDefineProperty':\\n115:           case 'ObjectDefineProperty':\\n116:             checkPropertyDescriptor(context, node.arguments[2]);\\n117:             break;\\n118:           default:\\n119:             throw new Error('Unreachable');\\n120:         }\\n121:       },\\n122: \\n123:       [`${CallExpression('ObjectCreate')}[arguments.length=2]`](node) {\\n124:         checkProperties(context, node.arguments[1]);\\n125:       },\\n126: \\n127:       [`:matches(${AnyFunction})[async=true]>BlockStatement ReturnStatement>ObjectExpression, :matches(${AnyFunction})[async=true]>ObjectExpression`](node) {\\n128:         if (node.parent.type === 'ReturnStatement') {\\n129:           let { parent } = node;\\n130:           do {\\n131:             ({ parent } = parent);\\n132:           } while (!parent.type.includes('Function'));\\n133: \\n134:           if (!parent.async) return;\\n135:         }\\n136:         if (isNullPrototypeObjectExpression(node) === false) {\\n137:           context.report({\\n138:             node,\\n139:             message: 'Use null-prototype when returning from async function',\\n140:           });\\n141:         }\\n142:       },\\n143: \\n144:       [CallExpression('RegExpPrototypeTest')](node) {\\n145:         context.report({\\n146:           node,\\n147:           message: '%RegExp.prototype.test% looks up the \\\"exec\\\" property of `this` value',\\n148:           suggest: [{\\n149:             desc: 'Use RegexpPrototypeExec instead',\\n150:             fix(fixer) {\\n151:               const testRange = [ ...node.range ];\\n152:               testRange[0] = testRange[0] + 'RegexpPrototype'.length;\\n153:               testRange[1] = testRange[0] + 'Test'.length;\\n154:               return [\\n155:                 fixer.replaceTextRange(testRange, 'Exec'),\\n156:                 fixer.insertTextAfter(node, ' !== null'),\\n157:               ];\\n158:             },\\n159:           }],\\n160:         });\\n161:       },\\n162:       [CallExpression(/^RegExpPrototypeSymbol(Match|MatchAll)$/)](node) {\\n163:         context.report({\\n164:           node,\\n165:           message: node.callee.name + ' looks up the \\\"exec\\\" property of `this` value',\\n166:         });\\n167:       },\\n168:       [CallExpression(/^(RegExpPrototypeSymbol|StringPrototype)Search$/)](node) {\\n169:         context.report({\\n170:           node,\\n171:           message: node.callee.name + ' is unsafe, use SafeStringPrototypeSearch instead',\\n172:         });\\n173:       },\\n174:       ...createUnsafeStringMethodReport(context, '%String.prototype.match%', 'Symbol.match'),\\n175:       ...createUnsafeStringMethodReport(context, '%String.prototype.matchAll%', 'Symbol.matchAll'),\\n176:       ...createUnsafeStringMethodOnRegexReport(context, '%String.prototype.replace%', 'Symbol.replace'),\\n177:       ...createUnsafeStringMethodOnRegexReport(context, '%String.prototype.replaceAll%', 'Symbol.replace'),\\n178:       ...createUnsafeStringMethodOnRegexReport(context, '%String.prototype.split%', 'Symbol.split'),\\n179: \\n180:       'NewExpression[callee.name=\\\"Proxy\\\"][arguments.1.type=\\\"ObjectExpression\\\"]'(node) {\\n181:         for (const { key, value } of node.arguments[1].properties) {\\n182:           if (\\n183:             key != null && value != null &&\\n184:             ((key.type === 'Identifier' && key.name === '__proto__') ||\\n185:               (key.type === 'Literal' && key.value === '__proto__')) &&\\n186:             value.type === 'Literal' && value.value === null\\n187:           ) {\\n188:             return;\\n189:           }\\n190:         }\\n191:         context.report({\\n192:           node,\\n193:           message: 'Proxy handler must be a null-prototype object',\\n194:         });\\n195:       },\\n196: \\n197:       [`ExpressionStatement>AwaitExpression>${CallExpression(/^(Safe)?PromiseAll(Settled)?$/)}`](node) {\\n198:         context.report({\\n199:           node,\\n200:           message: `Use          message: '%Promise.prototype.catch% looks up the `then` property of ' +\\n208:                    'the `this` argument, use PromisePrototypeThen instead',\\n209:         });\\n210:       },\\n211: \\n212:       [CallExpression('PromisePrototypeFinally')](node) {\\n213:         context.report({\\n214:           node,\\n215:           message: '%Promise.prototype.finally% looks up the `then` property of ' +\\n216:                    'the `this` argument, use SafePromisePrototypeFinally or ' +\\n217:                    'try/finally instead',\\n218:         });\\n219:       },\\n220: \\n221:       [CallExpression(/^Promise(All(Settled)?|Any|Race)/)](node) {\\n222:         context.report({\\n223:           node,\\n224:           message: `Use Safe${node.callee.name} instead of message: '%Array.prototype.concat% looks up `@@isConcatSpreadable` ' +\\n232:                    'which can be subject to prototype pollution',\\n233:         });\\n234:       },\\n235:     };\\n236:   },\\n237: };\\n238: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/nodejs/node@4d0cb65b33903033bc55bef26a30365caa755dd4/-/blob/tools/eslint-rules/avoid-prototype-pollution.js\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"32254089-148f-4a30-974b-7bc981ec8f2e","uuid":"df6063f0-ba96-43ff-8da5-42b78338db87","tool_use_result":{"content":"{\"content\":\"1: 'use strict';\\n2: \\n3: const CallExpression = (fnName) => `CallExpression[callee.name=${fnName}]`;\\n4: const AnyFunction = 'FunctionDeclaration, FunctionExpression, ArrowFunctionExpression';\\n5: \\n6: function checkProperties(context, node) {\\n7:   if (\\n8:     node.type === 'CallExpression' &&\\n9:     node.callee.name === 'ObjectGetOwnPropertyDescriptors'\\n10:   ) {\\n11:      fixer.insertTextBefore(node, '{ __proto__: null,...'),\\n60:             fixer.insertTextAfter(node, ' }'),\\n61:           ];\\n62:         },\\n63:       }],\\n64:     });\\n65:   }\\n66:   if `RegExpPrototypeSymbol${lookedUpProperty.charAt(dotPosition).toUpperCase()}${lookedUpProperty.slice(dotPosition + 1)}`;\\n90:   const lastDotPosition = '$String.prototype.'.length;\\n91:   const ];\\n158:             },\\n159:           }],\\n160:         });\\n161:       },\\n162:       [CallExpression(/^RegExpPrototypeSymbol(Match|MatchAll)$/)](node) {\\n163:         context.report({\\n164: node.callee.name + ' looks up the \"exec\" property of `this` value',\n166:         });\n167:       },\n168:       [CallExpression(/^(RegExpPrototypeSymbol|StringPrototype)Search$/)](node) {\n169: {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JABiCSwdfaR3zKfcT5NEcQ","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: 'use strict';\\n2: \\n3: /* eslint-disable node-core/prefer-primordials */\\n4: \\n5: // This file subclasses and stores the JS builtins that come from the VM\\n6: // so that Node.js's builtin modules do not need to later look these up from\\n7: // the global proxy, which can be mutated by users.\\n8: \\n9: // Use of primordials have sometimes a dramatic impact on performance, please\\n10: // benchmark all changes made in performance-sensitive areas of the codebase.\\n11: // See: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/38248\\n12: \\n13: const {\\n14:   defineProperty: ReflectDefineProperty,\\n15:   getOwnPropertyDescriptor: ReflectGetOwnPropertyDescriptor,\\n16:   ownKeys: ReflectOwnKeys,\\n17: } = Reflect;\\n18: \\n19: // `uncurryThis` is equivalent to `func => Function.prototype.call.bind(func)`.\\n20: // It is using `bind.bind(call)` to avoid using `Function.prototype.bind`\\n21: // and `Function.prototype.call` after it may have been mutated by users.\\n22: const { apply, bind, call } = Function.prototype;\\n23: const uncurryThis = bind.bind(call);\\n24: primordials.uncurryThis = uncurryThis;\\n25: \\n26: // `applyBind` is equivalent to `func => Function.prototype.apply.bind(func)`.\\n27: // It is using `bind.bind(apply)` to avoid using `Function.prototype.bind`\\n28: // and `Function.prototype.apply` after it may have been mutated by users.\\n29: const applyBind = bind.bind(apply);\\n30: primordials.applyBind = applyBind;\\n31: \\n32: // Methods that accept a variable number of arguments, and thus it's useful to\\n33: // also create `${prefix}${key}Apply`, which uses `Function.prototype.apply`,\\n34: // instead of `Function.prototype.call`, and thus doesn't require iterator\\n35: // destructuring.\\n36: const varargsMethods = [\\n37:   // 'ArrayPrototypeConcat' is omitted, because it performs the spread\\n38:   // on its own for arrays and array-likes with a truthy\\n39:   // @@isConcatSpreadable symbol property.\\n40:   'ArrayOf',\\n41:   'ArrayPrototypePush',\\n42:   'ArrayPrototypeUnshift',\\n43:   // 'FunctionPrototypeCall' is omitted, since there's 'ReflectApply'\\n44:   // and 'FunctionPrototypeApply'.\\n45:   'MathHypot',\\n46:   'MathMax',\\n47:   'MathMin',\\n48:   'StringFromCharCode',\\n49:   'StringFromCodePoint',\\n50:   'StringPrototypeConcat',\\n51:   'TypedArrayOf',\\n52: ];\\n53: \\n54: function getNewKey(key) {\\n55:   return typeof key === 'symbol' ?\\n56:     `Symbol${key.description[7].toUpperCase()}${key.description.slice(8)}` :\\n57:     `${key[0].toUpperCase()}${key.slice(1)}`;\\n58: }\\n59: \\n60: function copyAccessor(dest, prefix, key, { enumerable, get, set }) {\\n61:   ReflectDefineProperty(dest, `${prefix}Get${key}`, {\\n62:     __proto__: null,\\n63:     value: uncurryThis(get),\\n64:     enumerable,\\n65:   });\\n66:   if (set !== undefined) {\\n67:     ReflectDefineProperty(dest, `${prefix}Set${key}`, {\\n68:       __proto__: null,\\n69:       value: uncurryThis(set),\\n70:       enumerable,\\n71:     });\\n72:   }\\n73: }\\n74: \\n75: function copyPropsRenamed(src, dest, prefix) {\\n76:   for (const key of ReflectOwnKeys(src)) {\\n77:     const newKey = getNewKey(key);\\n78:     const desc = ReflectGetOwnPropertyDescriptor(src, key);\\n79:     if ('get' in desc) {\\n80:       copyAccessor(dest, prefix, newKey, desc);\\n81:     } else {\\n82:       const name = `${prefix}${newKey}`;\\n83:       ReflectDefineProperty(dest, name, { __proto__: null, ...desc });\\n84:       if (varargsMethods.includes(name)) {\\n85:         ReflectDefineProperty(dest, `${name}Apply`, {\\n86:           __proto__: null,\\n87:           // `src` is bound as the `this` so that the static `this` points\\n88:           // to the object it was defined on,\\n89:           // e.g.: `ArrayOfApply` gets a `this` of `Array`:\\n90:           value: applyBind(desc.value, src),\\n91:         });\\n92:       }\\n93:     }\\n94:   }\\n95: }\\n96: \\n97: function copyPropsRenamedBound(src, dest, prefix) {\\n98:   for (const key of ReflectOwnKeys(src)) {\\n99:     const newKey = getNewKey(key);\\n100:     const desc = ReflectGetOwnPropertyDescriptor(src, key);\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/nodejs/node@4d0cb65b33903033bc55bef26a30365caa755dd4/-/blob/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js%3FL1-100\"}"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"32254089-148f-4a30-974b-7bc981ec8f2e","uuid":"bdb7dc20-2626-4a75-96f2-d8bc25a0b550","tool_use_result":{"content":"{\"content\":\"1: 'use strict';\\n2: \\n3: /* eslint-disable node-core/prefer-primordials */\\n4: \\n5: // This file subclasses and stores the JS builtins that come from the VM\\n6: // so that Node.js's builtin modules do not need to later look these up from\\n7: // the global proxy, which can be mutated by users.\\n8: \\n9: // Use of primordials have sometimes a dramatic impact on performance, please\\n10: // benchmark all changes made in performance-sensitive areas of the codebase.\\n11: // See: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/38248\\n12: \\n13: const {\\n14:   defineProperty: ReflectDefineProperty,\\n15:   getOwnPropertyDescriptor: ReflectGetOwnPropertyDescriptor,\\n16:   ownKeys: ReflectOwnKeys,\\n17: } = Reflect;\\n18: \\n19: // `uncurryThis` is equivalent to `func => Function.prototype.call.bind(func)`.\\n20: // It is using `bind.bind(call)` to avoid using `Function.prototype.bind`\\n21: // and `Function.prototype.call` after it may have been mutated by users.\\n22: const { apply, bind, call } = Function.prototype;\\n23: const uncurryThis = bind.bind(call);\\n24: primordials.uncurryThis = uncurryThis;\\n25: \\n26: // `applyBind` is equivalent to `func => Function.prototype.apply.bind(func)`.\\n27: // It is using `bind.bind(apply)` to avoid using `Function.prototype.bind`\\n28: // and `Function.prototype.apply` after it may have been mutated by users.\\n29: const applyBind = bind.bind(apply);\\n30: primordials.applyBind = applyBind;\\n31: \\n32: // Methods that accept a variable number of arguments, and thus it's useful to\\n33: // also create `${prefix}${key}Apply`, which uses `Function.prototype.apply`,\\n34: // instead of `Function.prototype.call`, and thus doesn't require iterator\\n35: // destructuring.\\n36: const varargsMethods = [\\n37:   // 'ArrayPrototypeConcat' is omitted, because it performs the spread\\n38:   // on its own for arrays and array-likes with a truthy\\n39:   // @@isConcatSpreadable symbol property.\\n40:   'ArrayOf',\\n41:   'ArrayPrototypePush',\\n42:   'ArrayPrototypeUnshift',\\n43:   // 'FunctionPrototypeCall' is omitted, since there's 'ReflectApply'\\n44:   // and 'FunctionPrototypeApply'.\\n45:   'MathHypot',\\n46:   'MathMax',\\n47:   'MathMin',\\n48:   'StringFromCharCode',\\n49:   'StringFromCodePoint',\\n50:   'StringPrototypeConcat',\\n51:   'TypedArrayOf',\\n52: ];\\n53: \\n54: function getNewKey(key) {\\n55:   return typeof key === 'symbol' ?\\n56:     `Symbol${key.description[7].toUpperCase()}${key.description.slice(8)}` :\\n57:     `${key[0].toUpperCase()}${key.slice(1)}`;\\n58: }\\n59: \\n60: function copyAccessor(dest, prefix, key, { enumerable, get, set }) {\\n61:   ReflectDefineProperty(dest, `${prefix}Get${key}`, {\\n62:     __proto__: null,\\n63:     value: uncurryThis(get),\\n64:     enumerable,\\n65:   });\\n66:   if (set !== undefined) {\\n67:     ReflectDefineProperty(dest, `${prefix}Set${key}`, {\\n68:       __proto__: null,\\n69:       value: uncurryThis(set),\\n70:       enumerable,\\n71:     });\\n72:   }\\n73: }\\n74: \\n75: function copyPropsRenamed(src, dest, prefix) {\\n76:   for (const key of ReflectOwnKeys(src)) {\\n77:     const newKey = getNewKey(key);\\n78:     const desc = ReflectGetOwnPropertyDescriptor(src, key);\\n79:     if ('get' in desc) {\\n80:       copyAccessor(dest, prefix, newKey, desc);\\n81:     } else {\\n82:       const name = `${prefix}${newKey}`;\\n83:       ReflectDefineProperty(dest, name, { __proto__: null, ...desc });\\n84:       if (varargsMethods.includes(name)) {\\n85:         ReflectDefineProperty(dest, `${name}Apply`, {\\n86:           __proto__: null,\\n87:           // `src` is bound as the `this` so that the static `this` points\\n88:           // to the object it was defined on,\\n89:           // e.g.: `ArrayOfApply` gets a `this` of `Array`:\\n90:           value: applyBind(desc.value, src),\\n91:         });\\n92:       }\\n93:     }\\n94:   }\\n95: }\\n96: \\n97: function copyPropsRenamedBound(src, dest, prefix) {\\n98:   for (const key of ReflectOwnKeys(src)) {\\n99:     const newKey = getNewKey(key);\\n100:     const desc = ReflectGetOwnPropertyDescriptor(src, key);\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/nodejs/node@4d0cb65b33903033bc55bef26a30365caa755dd4/-/blob/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js%3FL1-100\"}","structuredContent":{"content":"1: 'use strict';\n2: \n3: /* eslint-disable node-core/prefer-primordials */\n4: \n5: // This file subclasses and stores the JS builtins that come from the VM\n6: // so that Node.js's builtin modules do not need to later look these up from\n7: // the global proxy, which can be mutated by users.\n8: \n9: // Use of primordials have sometimes a dramatic impact on performance, please\n10: // benchmark all changes made in performance-sensitive areas of the codebase.\n11: // See: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/38248\n12: \n13: const {\n14:   defineProperty: ReflectDefineProperty,\n15:   getOwnPropertyDescriptor: ReflectGetOwnPropertyDescriptor,\n16:   ownKeys: ReflectOwnKeys,\n17: } = Reflect;\n18: \n19: // `uncurryThis` is equivalent to `func => Function.prototype.call.bind(func)`.\n20: // It is using `bind.bind(call)` to avoid using `Function.prototype.bind`\n21: // and `Function.prototype.call` after it may have been mutated by users.\n22: const { apply, bind, call } = Function.prototype;\n23: const uncurryThis = bind.bind(call);\n24: primordials.uncurryThis = uncurryThis;\n25: \n26: // `applyBind` is equivalent to `func => Function.prototype.apply.bind(func)`.\n27: // It is using `bind.bind(apply)` to avoid using `Function.prototype.bind`\n28: // and `Function.prototype.apply` after it may have been mutated by users.\n29: const applyBind = bind.bind(apply);\n30: primordials.applyBind = applyBind;\n31: \n32: // Methods that accept a variable number of arguments, and thus it's useful to\n33: // also create `${prefix}${key}Apply`, which uses `Function.prototype.apply`,\n34: // instead of `Function.prototype.call`, and thus doesn't require iterator\n35: // destructuring.\n36: const varargsMethods = [\n37:   // 'ArrayPrototypeConcat' is omitted, because it performs the spread\n38:   // on its own for arrays and array-likes with a truthy\n39:   // @@isConcatSpreadable symbol property.\n40:   'ArrayOf',\n41:   'ArrayPrototypePush',\n42:   'ArrayPrototypeUnshift',\n43:   // 'FunctionPrototypeCall' is omitted, since there's 'ReflectApply'\n44:   // and 'FunctionPrototypeApply'.\n45:   'MathHypot',\n46:   'MathMax',\n47:   'MathMin',\n48:   'StringFromCharCode',\n49:   'StringFromCodePoint',\n50:   'StringPrototypeConcat',\n51:   'TypedArrayOf',\n52: ];\n53: \n54: function getNewKey(key) {\n55:   return typeof key === 'symbol' ?\n56:     `Symbol${key.description[7].toUpperCase()}${key.description.slice(8)}` :\n57:     `${key[0].toUpperCase()}${key.slice(1)}`;\n58: }\n59: \n60: function copyAccessor(dest, prefix, key, { enumerable, get, set }) {\n61:   ReflectDefineProperty(dest, `${prefix}Get${key}`, {\n62:     __proto__: null,\n63:     value: uncurryThis(get),\n64:     enumerable,\n65:   });\n66:   if (set !== undefined) {\n67:     ReflectDefineProperty(dest, `${prefix}Set${key}`, {\n68:       __proto__: null,\n69:       value: uncurryThis(set),\n70:       enumerable,\n71:     });\n72:   }\n73: }\n74: \n75: function copyPropsRenamed(src, dest, prefix) {\n76:   for (const key of ReflectOwnKeys(src)) {\n77:     const newKey = getNewKey(key);\n78:     const desc = ReflectGetOwnPropertyDescriptor(src, key);\n79:     if ('get' in desc) {\n80:       copyAccessor(dest, prefix, newKey, desc);\n81:     } else {\n82:       const name = `${prefix}${newKey}`;\n83:       ReflectDefineProperty(dest, name, { __proto__: null, ...desc });\n84:       if (varargsMethods.includes(name)) {\n85:         ReflectDefineProperty(dest, `${name}Apply`, {\n86:           __proto__: null,\n87:           // `src` is bound as the `this` so that the static `this` points\n88:           // to the object it was defined on,\n89:           // e.g.: `ArrayOfApply` gets a `this` of `Array`:\n90:           value: applyBind(desc.value, src),\n91:         });\n92:       }\n93:     }\n94:   }\n95: }\n96: \n97: function copyPropsRenamedBound(src, dest, prefix) {\n98:   for (const key of ReflectOwnKeys(src)) {\n99:     const newKey = getNewKey(key);\n100:     const desc = ReflectGetOwnPropertyDescriptor(src, key);\n101: The resolved value cannot be modified from the callback.\n441:  * Prefer using async functions when possible.\n442:  * @param {Promise<any>} thisPromise\n443:  * @param {(() => void) | undefined | null} onFinally The callback to execute\n444:  *   when the Promise is settled (fulfilled or rejected).\n445:  * @returns {Promise} A Promise for the completion of the callback.\n446:  */\n447: primordials.SafePromisePrototypeFinally = (thisPromise, onFinally) =>\n448:   // Wrapping on a new Promise is necessary to not expose the SafePromise\n449:   // prototype to user-land.\n450:   new Promise((a, b) =>\n451: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/nodejs/node@4d0cb65b33903033bc55bef26a30365caa755dd4/-/blob/lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js%3FL350-450"}}}

\"repo\": \"github.com/nodejs/node\",\n      \"path\": \"lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js\",\n      \"symbol\": \"SafeMap\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/nodejs/node\",\n \"lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js\",\n      \"symbol\": \"SafeArrayIterator\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/nodejs/node\",\n      \"path\": \"lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js\",\n      \"symbol\": \"SafePromiseAll\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/nodejs/node\",\n      \"path\": \"lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js\",\n      \"symbol\": \"SafePromiseAny\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/nodejs/node\",\n      \"path\": \"lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js\",\n      \"symbol\": \"SafePromisePrototypeFinally\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/nodejs/node\",\n      \"path\": \"tools/eslint-rules/avoid-prototype-pollution.js\",\n      \"symbol\": \"checkPropertyDescriptor\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/nodejs/node\",\n      \"path\": \"tools/eslint-rules/avoid-prototype-pollution.js\",\n      \"symbol\": \"isNullPrototypeObjectExpression\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/nodejs/node\",\n      \"path\": \"lib/buffer.js\",\n      \"symbol\": \"ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"repo\": \"github.com/nodejs/node\",\n      \"path\": \"lib/internal/util/inspect.js\",\n      \"symbol\": \"ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"text\": \"Node.js core implements comprehensive prototype pollution defenses across its JavaScript modules using multiple complementary strategies:\\n\\n## 1. Primordials-Based Protection (lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js)\\n\\nThe cornerstone of Node's defense is the primordials system, which creates safe, frozen wrappers of built-in objects to prevent prototype pollution attacks. Key components include:\\n\\n- **makeSafe()** function: Creates safe subclasses of built-in constructors (Map, Set, WeakMap, WeakSet, Promise, etc.) by freezing their prototypes and setting them to null via ObjectSetPrototypeOf(safe.prototype, null)\\n- **Safe* Classes**: SafeMap, SafeSet, SafeWeakMap, SafeWeakSet, SafeArrayIterator, and Safe Promise variants that replace their unsafe counterparts throughout the codebase\\n- **Object Definition**: Uses ReflectDefineProperty with `{ __proto__: null, ...desc }` pattern to safely define properties, preventing prototype chain attacks\\n\\n## 2. Null-Prototype Object Pattern\\n\\nNumerous core modules use `{ __proto__: null }` object literals for safe property storage:\\n- lib/v8.js, lib/vm.js, lib/quic.js: Use null-prototype objects for constants and configuration\\n- lib/_http_agent.js, lib/_http_outgoing.js: Safely store HTTP options with `{ __proto__: null, ...options }`\\n- lib/internal/webidl.js, lib/internal/options.js: Safe dictionary creation with null prototypes\\n- lib/internal/util/inspect.js, lib/internal/heap_utils.js, lib/internal/event_target.js: Property definitions with null prototypes\\n\\n## 3. ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty Pattern\\n\\nCore modules defensively use ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty for safe property checks:\\n- lib/buffer.js, lib/cluster.js, lib/internal/tty.js: Check own properties to avoid inherited prototype properties\\n- lib/child_process.js, lib/internal/errors.js: Validate object properties without triggering prototype lookups\\n- lib/internal/util/inspect.js, lib/internal/validators.js: Safe property iteration and checking\\n- lib/internal/crypto/util.js, lib/internal/crypto/webidl.js: Secure algorithm/parameter validation\\n\\n## 4. ObjectCreate(null) with Safe Properties\\n\\nFiles that create null-prototype objects with protected properties:\\n- lib/quic.js: Uses ObjectCreate(null, { __proto__: null, ...properties })\\n- lib/internal/error_serdes.js: Creates `const all = { __proto__: null }` for error property serialization\\n- lib/internal/console/global.js: ReflectDefineProperty with null-prototype descriptors\\n- lib/internal/worker/io.js: Safely creates property descriptors with null prototypes\\n\\n## 5. Prototype Chain Hardening with ObjectSetPrototypeOf\\n\\nCore modules set safe prototypes explicitly:\\n- lib/net.js, lib/dgram.js: `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(Server.prototype, EventEmitter.prototype)`\\n- lib/buffer.js: `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(Buffer, Uint8Array)`\\n- lib/internal/per_context/domexception.js: `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(safe.prototype, null)`\\n- lib/internal/freeze_intrinsics.js: Deep freezing of intrinsic objects to prevent prototype pollution\\n\\n## 6. ESLint Enforcement\\n\\ntools/eslint-rules/avoid-prototype-pollution.js enforces:\\n- Null-prototype objects for property descriptors\\n- Safe primordial usage patterns\\n- Avoidance of unsafe built-in methods that lookup prototype properties\\n- Proxy handler null-prototype requirement\\n\\n## 7. High-Level Module Protection\\n\\nCluster, worker, test runner, and stream modules universally use Safe* versions:\\n- lib/internal/cluster/: Uses SafeMap, SafeSet for handle and worker tracking\\n- lib/internal/test_runner/: SafeMap for test assertions, SafeSet for tracking\\n- lib/internal/streams/: ObjectSetPrototypeOf patterns for stream hierarchy\\n- lib/internal/perf/: SafeMap for performance metrics and user timing\\n\\nThis multi-layered approach ensures that user-controlled input cannot pollute the prototype chain of critical system objects, protecting against both direct and indirect prototype pollution attacks throughout Node.js core.\"\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3475,"cache_read_input_tokens":81052,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3475},"output_tokens":6,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"32254089-148f-4a30-974b-7bc981ec8f2e","uuid":"dcc77b20-9ad1-4585-b359-5a128ffb6e83"}

\"symbol\": \"ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"text\": \"Node.js core implements comprehensive prototype pollution defenses across its JavaScript modules using multiple complementary strategies:\\n\\n## 1. Primordials-Based Protection (lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js)\\n\\nThe cornerstone of Node's defense is the primordials system, which creates safe, frozen wrappers of built-in objects to prevent prototype pollution attacks. Key components include:\\n\\n- **makeSafe()** function: Creates safe subclasses of built-in constructors (Map, Set, WeakMap, WeakSet, Promise, etc.) by freezing their prototypes and setting them to null via ObjectSetPrototypeOf(safe.prototype, null)\\n- **Safe* Classes**: SafeMap, SafeSet, SafeWeakMap, SafeWeakSet, SafeArrayIterator, and Safe Promise variants that replace their unsafe counterparts throughout the codebase\\n- **Object Definition**: Uses ReflectDefineProperty with `{ __proto__: null, ...desc }` pattern to safely define properties, preventing prototype chain attacks\\n\\n## 2. Null-Prototype Object Pattern\\n\\nNumerous core modules use `{ __proto__: null }` object literals for safe property storage:\\n- lib/v8.js, lib/vm.js, lib/quic.js: Use null-prototype objects for constants and configuration\\n- lib/_http_agent.js, lib/_http_outgoing.js: Safely store HTTP options with `{ __proto__: null, ...options }`\\n- lib/internal/webidl.js, lib/internal/options.js: Safe dictionary creation with null prototypes\\n- lib/internal/util/inspect.js, lib/internal/heap_utils.js, lib/internal/event_target.js: Property definitions with null prototypes\\n\\n## 3. ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty Pattern\\n\\nCore modules defensively use ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty for safe property checks:\\n- lib/buffer.js, lib/cluster.js, lib/internal/tty.js: Check own properties to avoid inherited prototype properties\\n- lib/child_process.js, lib/internal/errors.js: Validate object properties without triggering prototype lookups\\n- lib/internal/util/inspect.js, lib/internal/validators.js: Safe property iteration and checking\\n- lib/internal/crypto/util.js, lib/internal/crypto/webidl.js: Secure algorithm/parameter validation\\n\\n## 4. ObjectCreate(null) with Safe Properties\\n\\nFiles that create null-prototype objects with protected properties:\\n- lib/quic.js: Uses ObjectCreate(null, { __proto__: null, ...properties })\\n- lib/internal/error_serdes.js: Creates `const all = { __proto__: null }` for error property serialization\\n- lib/internal/console/global.js: ReflectDefineProperty with null-prototype descriptors\\n- lib/internal/worker/io.js: Safely creates property descriptors with null prototypes\\n\\n## 5. Prototype Chain Hardening with ObjectSetPrototypeOf\\n\\nCore modules set safe prototypes explicitly:\\n- lib/net.js, lib/dgram.js: `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(Server.prototype, EventEmitter.prototype)`\\n- lib/buffer.js: `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(Buffer, Uint8Array)`\\n- lib/internal/per_context/domexception.js: `ObjectSetPrototypeOf(safe.prototype, null)`\\n- lib/internal/freeze_intrinsics.js: Deep freezing of intrinsic objects to prevent prototype pollution\\n\\n## 6. ESLint Enforcement\\n\\ntools/eslint-rules/avoid-prototype-pollution.js enforces:\\n- Null-prototype objects for property descriptors\\n- Safe primordial usage patterns\\n- Avoidance of unsafe built-in methods that lookup prototype properties\\n- Proxy handler null-prototype requirement\\n\\n## 7. High-Level Module Protection\\n\\nCluster, worker, test runner, and stream modules universally use Safe* versions:\\n- lib/internal/cluster/: Uses SafeMap, SafeSet for handle and worker tracking\\n- lib/internal/test_runner/: SafeMap for test assertions, SafeSet for tracking\\n- lib/internal/streams/: ObjectSetPrototypeOf patterns for stream hierarchy\\n- lib/internal/perf/: SafeMap for performance metrics and user timing\\n\\nThis multi-layered approach ensures that user-controlled input cannot pollute the prototype chain of critical system objects, protecting against both direct and indirect prototype pollution attacks throughout Node.js core.\"\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_011Kynp7Jw3ih2CK9WMSwdsb","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I've completed a comprehensive analysis of prototype pollution defense mechanisms in Node.js core JavaScript modules. Here's what I found:\n\n## Summary\n\nI discovered **92 JavaScript source files** in `nodejs/node` that implement prototype pollution defenses using multiple complementary strategies:\n\n### Key Defense Patterns:\n\n1. **Primordials System** (`lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js`): Safe wrappers for built-in objects with frozen, null-prototype implementations (SafeMap, SafeSet, SafeWeakMap, SafePromise variants, etc.)\n\n2. **Null-Prototype Objects** (`{ __proto__: null }`): Used across 30+ files for safe property storage in HTTP agents, configuration objects, and utility modules\n\n3. **ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty Checks**: Defensive property validation in buffer, cluster, crypto, and validation modules to prevent inherited property lookups\n\n4. **ObjectCreate(null)**: Creates objects without prototype chains for safe configuration and error serialization\n\n5. **ObjectSetPrototypeOf Hardening**: Explicit prototype chain control in network, stream, and worker modules\n\n6. **ESLint Enforcement**: `tools/eslint-rules/avoid-prototype-pollution.js` enforces patterns and flags unsafe built-in method calls\n\n### Coverage:\n\nThe defenses are consistently applied across all major core modules including:\n- HTTP modules (_http_agent.js, _http_server.js, _http_client.js)\n- Network modules (net.js, dgram.js, tls)\n- Stream modules (transform, duplex, passthrough)\n- Worker/cluster modules\n- File system modules\n- Test runner and performance monitoring\n- Crypto and WebIDL modules\n\nThe answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with complete file paths, key symbols, and a detailed narrative explanation."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":6260,"cache_read_input_tokens":84527,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":6260},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"32254089-148f-4a30-974b-7bc981ec8f2e","uuid":"ebe7855d-c95e-4f4c-b3d4-234eca9cf02f"}
{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":68987,"duration_api_ms":65822,"num_turns":23,"result":"I've completed a comprehensive analysis of prototype pollution defense mechanisms in Node.js core JavaScript modules. Here's what I found:\n\n## Summary\n\nI discovered **92 JavaScript source files** in `nodejs/node` that implement prototype pollution defenses using multiple complementary strategies:\n\n### Key Defense Patterns:\n\n1. **Primordials System** (`lib/internal/per_context/primordials.js`): Safe wrappers for built-in objects with frozen, null-prototype implementations (SafeMap, SafeSet, SafeWeakMap, SafePromise variants, etc.)\n\n2. **Null-Prototype Objects** (`{ __proto__: null }`): Used across 30+ files for safe property storage in HTTP agents, configuration objects, and utility modules\n\n3. **ObjectPrototypeHasOwnProperty Checks**: Defensive property validation in buffer, cluster, crypto, and validation modules to prevent inherited property lookups\n\n4. **ObjectCreate(null)**: Creates objects without prototype chains for safe configuration and error serialization\n\n5. **ObjectSetPrototypeOf Hardening**: Explicit prototype chain control in network, stream, and worker modules\n\n6. **ESLint Enforcement**: `tools/eslint-rules/avoid-prototype-pollution.js` enforces patterns and flags unsafe built-in method calls\n\n### Coverage:\n\nThe defenses are consistently applied across all major core modules including:\n- HTTP modules (_http_agent.js, _http_server.js, _http_client.js)\n- Network modules (net.js, dgram.js, tls)\n- Stream modules (transform, duplex, passthrough)\n- Worker/cluster modules\n- File system modules\n- Test runner and performance monitoring\n- Crypto and WebIDL modules\n\nThe answer has been saved to
